reason: bomull = cotton (plant and textile). The sub-definition is a definition of the word, not a separate sense. Single meaning; clear the stale list. Uncountable noun.
current 'drill, to bore' embeds 'to' — app adds 'to', so it would render 'to drill, to to bore'. Remove the explicit 'to'.
borsta: primary 'brush' is correct. Secondary 'to booze' has explicit 'to' — fix phrasing in list.
boskap = livestock (all farm animals), which is broader than just cattle. 'Livestock' is the better primary; 'cattle' is a near-synonym. Both are uncountable. Merge as primary; single sense.
bostadsrätt has two genuine senses: (1) the apartment/dwelling itself, (2) the legal right of tenancy. The sub-definition as written is definition-like. Rebuild cleanly.
box: one sub-definition 'a cuboid container' is a definition of 'box', not a separate sense. The genuine second sense is 'blow with the fist'. Fix the list.
bredda: two real senses — make physically wider, and broaden figuratively. Current sub-definition is phrased as a definition. Clean up.
brevlåda: two senses — delivery point at home vs collection post box on the street. Current sub-definition is definition-like. Rebuild with the delivery sense as primary (more everyday).
senses: mailbox, letterbox (delivery point at your door)
post box (collection point on the street)

reason: bricka: 'card' in the current list is vague — bricka means a tile or token (e.g. board-game piece), not a playing card. Clarify that sense.

broms = brake (primary). Also means horsefly — a common everyday meaning missing from the current list. 'Something that slows progress' is figurative extension of brake, not a separate lexical sense worth listing separately.

brytning: primary 'break, a breaking, a rupture' is clumsy and embeds an article. Everyday senses are accent (foreign) and mining/extraction. Neither clearly dominates; use semicolon. List all four senses.
brådskande: current primary is a definition ('needing to be done soon'). 'Urgent' is the clean one-word gloss.
bråkdel: 'fraction' is correct. Sub-definition 'a fractional part (of a mixed number)' is a restatement/definition, not a distinct sense. Single meaning.
bråttom is an adverb used in 'ha bråttom' (to be in a hurry). Current gloss 'urgency due to lack of time, hurry' is definition-like. Better phrase: 'in a hurry' conveys the usage naturally.
bräda: core meaning is a plank/board (piece of wood). Current sub-definitions are muddled. Rebuild cleanly with the two real senses.
brödraskap: 'close friendship between men or boys' is a paraphrase of brotherhood, not a genuinely distinct sense. Single meaning.
bubbla: 'a Bug, a Beetle (VW car)' is very marginal slang. Keep the two main senses: bubble (physical) and bubble (financial).
bukt: sub-definition 'bend, loop' is phrased adequately but the overall list can be cleaner.
senses: bend, loop (in a path or rope)

reason: bulle: current 'bun, a small bread roll' embeds an article. Fix phrasing. Keep the secondary senses.

buller: current primary 'unpleasant noise' is a definition. The clean translation is 'noise, din'. Uncountable. The second sense 'loud rumble' is a specific subset, not a genuinely distinct sense — drop it.
bunt: current 'bundle, a bunch' embeds an article. Fix. Single meaning.
bur: current sub-definition 'a prison, a jail, an arrest' embeds articles. Clean up.
bäck: current 'small stream or brook' is a definition. 'Brook, stream' is the clean bare translation.
bädda: primary 'make' is too bare — it only means 'make' in the fixed phrase 'make the bed'. The true primary is 'make the bed'. Secondary senses: tuck in, pave the way for.
bägare: current 'cup, a beaker, a goblet' embeds an article. Fix phrasing. Secondary sense (container for e.g. ice cream) is real.
charm: the sub-definition is a definition of the word, not a separate sense. Single meaning. Uncountable.
cyklist: current 'cyclist, one who rides a bicycle' — 'one who rides a bicycle' is a definition of cyclist. Single meaning.
dagsljus: primary 'daylight' is fine (uncountable). The figurative second sense (public exposure) is real. Fix the list.
darra: current 'shiver, to tremble' embeds 'to' — app adds 'to'. Fix.
deckare: current 'crime novel, a whodunit' embeds an article. Fix. Keep both senses (crime novel and detective).
deg: sub-definition 'a thick mix of flour and water' is a definition of dough, not a distinct sense. The slang sense 'dough (money)' is real and should be kept. Fix list.
diamant: sub-definition 'a diamond (gemstone)' is a restatement of the primary — not a distinct sense. Single meaning.
dike: sub-definition 'a small canal, for irrigation or drainage' is a definition of ditch. Single meaning.
diktera: both senses are genuine. Sub-definitions had clumsy parentheticals. Rebuild cleanly.
senses: dictate (speak for someone to write down)

reason: disponera: current primary 'have disposal' is clumsy. Three real senses. Neither of the first two clearly dominates, so use semicolon.
senses: have use of (be able to use without owning)

reason: djärv: 'venturesome' is marginal and old-fashioned. 'Bold, daring' is sufficient and clean.
docka: current 'doll, a puppet' embeds an article. Fix. Two genuine senses.

dragning: in everyday Swedish, dragning most commonly means a draw (lottery) or an attraction/inclination towards something. Current 'drawing, drag' is vague. Neither sense clearly dominates; use semicolon.
drake: primary 'dragon' is correct. The keelboat and fierce-woman senses are too marginal. Keep: dragon, kite, dragon ship, drake (duck).
drastisk: sub-definition 'drastically' is the adverb form, not a sense of this adjective. Drop it.
droppa: two Wiktionary senses are essentially the same action phrased differently. Single meaning; clear the list.
dräkt: 'outer form' is an archaic/literary figurative sense; 'a draw, a pull' appears to be an error (that is dragning). Drop those. Core meanings: costume/dress and suit.
duscha: current sub-definitions include restatements of the primary. The genuine second sense is 'shower (spray someone with something)'. Fix list.
duva: 'dove, friend of peace' is a figurative extension of the dove sense, not a wholly separate meaning. But the figurative 'dove' (pacifist) is common enough in political contexts to list.
dåtid: two genuine senses — the past (time period) and past tense (grammar). Neither clearly dominates. Use semicolon.
effektivisera: current 'make something more efficient' is definition-like. 'Streamline' is clean and common; add 'make more efficient' as clarifier.
eftergift: two genuine senses — concession (yielding to demands) and waiver/remission (legal/financial). Current sub-definition phrasing is acceptable but can be cleaner.
efterlevnad: 'compliance' is the more common English term in regulatory/legal contexts; 'adherence' is also valid. Combine both. Uncountable.
